Bokeh photography term that describes blurry, out-of-focus points light portions photograph.
This type photographic composition become more popular with advent image editing mobile device photography apps.
Most often, photograph using bokeh effect renders distant parts photo fuzzy, soft focus.
This blurring makes subject, usually foreground, focal point.
Having sharp, solid feature serve contrast dreamy, blurred background brings attention that feature.
Bokeh photography striking when shooting portrait.
When shooting bokeh with camera lens, photographer must attention aperture, shutter speed depth field, blurring portions composition that farther away from camera.
photograph featuring bokeh usually involves shallow depth field.
darkroom most part fallen wayside, digital photography ability accomplish editing programs such Photoshop made technique bokeh available average person.
There many tutorials photo tips online.
term bokeh borrowed from Japanese, where word rendered bo-ke.
Japanese, bo-ke means haze blur, also used mean brain senility age.
term came into English language 1990s.
Mike Johnston, editor Photo Techniques magazine 1997, suggested spelling that currently used today, encourage correct pronunciation term.
Bokeh pronounced BO-kay.
